executor_handoff.system_instructions: Preserve existing patterns. Follow scope exactly. Do not widen scope. Use chosen path only. Stay in ValistreamCore + Valistream CLI targets. Serena for edits. xcode-tools for build/test validation.

task_brief.objective: Add spec citation to every spec-grounded Finding. New field Finding.specRef: String?. Human cite like "RFC 8216 §6.2.2" / "HLS Authoring §9.1". Surface in ALL output sinks: report.md, report.json, findings.jsonl, --json stdout, AND human console finding line. Operational/classification findings carry NO cite (specRef = nil).
task_brief.why: "TOOL.staleness" etc. tell a backend/engineer nothing about WHY it's wrong. A spec chapter ref justifies the finding and lets the streaming backend map issues.
task_brief.repo_signals: - Finding model: ValistreamCore/Validation/Finding.swift. Already has ruleId, source, severity, category. Codable, Sendable, Equatable. - ruleId already encodes rule identity (RFC8216.* / APPLE.* / TOOL.*). It is ALSO the de-dup key (ValidationSession.swift:547 hashes resource|ruleId|line|message). DO NOT change ruleId values. - All JSON sinks encode Finding via Finding.jsonEncoder (sortedKeys). So a stored Codable field reaches report.json + findings.jsonl + --json with zero per-sink work. - report.md finding render: SessionReportBuilder.findingSubsection (line ~495) emits "- Rule: `\(finding.ruleId)`". - Console human render: StatusRenderer.swift findingLines (~386-389) builds "marker outcome snapshot: message". Console shows NO ruleId today. - Rule construction sites pass ruleId into Finding/RuleViolation across: Validation/Rules/*, Monitoring/ContinuityChecker.swift, Monitoring/StalenessDetector.swift, Validation/StreamClassifier.swift, Session/PlaylistLoader.swift, Session/ValidationSession+Reporting.swift, Session/ValidationSession.swift.
task_brief.scope: IN: - New stored property Finding.specRef: String? (Codable, auto-included). - New SpecCatalog mapping ruleId -> citation (single source of truth). - Finding gets specRef derived at init from SpecCatalog (covers ALL construction sites, one edit). - report.md: append " (specRef)" after the backticked ruleId when non-nil. - console human finding line: append " (specRef)" to message text when non-nil. - Update tests (schema, finding, markdown, console golden outputs, conformance). OUT: - No ruleId value changes. No source/severity/category changes. No new CLI flags. - No reclassification of findings between sources. No split standard/section JSON (single string only).
task_brief.implementation_path:
Single path: derive specRef inside Finding.init via SpecCatalog. NO new init parameter; NO per-site edits; NO stamping in record().
1. SpecCatalog (new file Validation/SpecCatalog.swift): enum SpecCatalog { static func reference(forRuleId id: String) -> String? } - If id has prefix "RFC8216.": take remainder, drop everything from first "-" onward, return "RFC 8216 §". RFC8216.4.3.3.1 -> "RFC 8216 §4.3.3.1" RFC8216.4.3.3.1-DURATION -> "RFC 8216 §4.3.3.1" RFC8216.4.3.3-DUPLICATE -> "RFC 8216 §4.3.3" RFC8216.4.3.4.2-BANDWIDTH/-URI -> "RFC 8216 §4.3.4.2" - Else explicit dictionary (below). - Else nil. 2. Finding.swift: add `public let specRef: String?`. In memberwise init set `self.specRef = SpecCatalog.reference(forRuleId: ruleId)`. Do NOT add a specRef param. Codable stays synthesized; Optional encodes via encodeIfPresent => nil => key omitted in JSON (good for backend). 3. report.md (SessionReportBuilder.findingSubsection ~line 495): let ref = finding.specRef.map { " (\($0))" } ?? "" md += "- Rule: `\(finding.ruleId)`\(ref)\n" 4. Console (StatusRenderer): append " (\(specRef))" to the finding message string in BOTH render paths — live findingLines (~386-389) and the summary formatFinding (~164-165). Bare when nil.
task_brief.code_level_guidance:
EXPLICIT DICTIONARY (ruleId -> specRef): APPLE.codecs -> "HLS Authoring §9.1" APPLE.resolution -> "HLS Authoring §9.2" APPLE.independent-segments -> "HLS Authoring §9.11" APPLE.average-bandwidth -> "HLS Authoring §9.14" APPLE.iframe-playlists -> "HLS Authoring §6.1" TOOL.continuity.media-sequence -> "RFC 8216 §6.2.2" TOOL.continuity.head-removal -> "RFC 8216 §6.2.2" TOOL.continuity.segment-stability -> "RFC 8216 §6.2.2" TOOL.continuity.discontinuity-inserted -> "RFC 8216 §4.3.2.3" TOOL.continuity.discontinuity-sequence -> "RFC 8216 §4.3.3.3" TOOL.staleness -> "RFC 8216 §6.2.2"
BARE (NOT in dict, NOT RFC-derivable => nil): APPLE.variant-ladder, APPLE.target-duration, TOOL.low-latency, TOOL.encryption, TOOL.delivery, TOOL.selection-empty, TOOL.selection-empty, and any future TOOL.* not listed.
NOTE: APPLE.variant-ladder + APPLE.target-duration are intentionally bare (decided). Do not invent cites. Source file must be UTF-8; "§" literal is required exactly. RFC derivation must run for ALL RFC8216.* ids incl ones not enumerated above.
task_brief.entry_points: - ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Validation/Finding.swift (add field + init derive) - ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Validation/SpecCatalog.swift (NEW) - ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/SessionReportBuilder.swift (~495 md Rule line) - Valistream/Valistream/StatusRenderer.swift (~386-389 live, ~164-165 summary) Tests likely needing updates: - ValistreamCoreTests/Validation/FindingTests.swift, JSONEncoderTests.swift - ValistreamCoreTests/Session/ReportJSONSchemaTests.swift (add specRef key; bump schema if versioned) - ValistreamCoreTests/Session/ReportMarkdownTests.swift, SessionReportTests.swift - ValistreamCoreTests/Conformance/*ViolationTests.swift (may assert specRef alongside ruleId) - NEW ValistreamCoreTests/Validation/SpecCatalogTests.swift - Integration golden-output tests (console lines) under Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/: QuietModeFindingsTests, EvidenceInOutputTests, NormalSessionReadabilityTests, VerbosityEquivalenceTests, VerboseDistinctnessTests, NonInteractiveOutputTests, LiveFaultScenarioTests, IncidentTimelineReportTests, and any asserting finding lines.
task_brief.search_fallback: grep "finding.ruleId" and "- Rule:" for md render. grep "func findingLines", "formatFinding" for console render. grep "schemaVersion" or "version" in ReportJSONSchemaTests for schema bump. After change grep "specRef" to confirm all sinks + tests covered.
task_brief.acceptance_criteria:
- Finding.specRef non-nil and exactly matches table for every spec-grounded ruleId; nil for bare set. - RFC8216.* (incl -SUFFIX variants) derive correctly to "RFC 8216 §
". - report.md: spec-grounded finding shows "- Rule: `` ()"; bare shows "- Rule: ``". - report.json / findings.jsonl / --json: specRef key present only when non-nil (encodeIfPresent), value = cite string. - console human finding line: appends " ()" when non-nil; unchanged when nil. - ruleId values unchanged; de-dup identity unaffected. - SpecCatalogTests cover: RFC derive (plain + suffix), each APPLE/TOOL mapped id, a bare id => nil.
task_brief.validation: Build Valistream scheme (xcode-tools BuildProject). Run Valistream.xctestplan (RunAllTests). Expect golden-output console/markdown/JSON-schema tests to need expected-string updates — update them to include the cite, do not weaken assertions. Sanity: re-run against a TOOL.staleness-heavy report; finding lines + report.md + report.json all show "RFC 8216 §6.2.2".
